Name: Global Administrators that signed in during the last 14 days
Description:
This indicator looks for Global Administrators that have signed in during the past 14 days.

Required permissions

  • RoleManagement.Read.All
  • User.Read.All
  • AuditLog.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Users that hold the Global Administrator role are the most privileged users in Entra ID. Organizations should work to limit the necessity of using a Global Administrator, such as using less permissive roles when possible.

An attacker will find users with the Global Administrator role as high-valued, and a compromised Global Administrator can lead to several attacks against the organization, that can include data exfiltration, lateral movement into other systems in Azure, multi-cloud, and on-premise systems, and can result in complete tenant compromise where an organization becomes locked out of their Entra tenant by the attacker.

References

Microsoft Entra built-in roles - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: Found {0} from {1} Global Administrators that signed in (interactive or non interactive) in the last 14 days.
Remediation:
Review the Global Administrators listed in the results of this indicator.

If an organization believes that there is highly suspicious activity or a confirmed compromise of a Global Administrator, they must treat this as a critical security event.

If this account is believed to be suspicious, the Audit logs for the account activity can be viewed in Entra ID.

To view the audit logs, open the Microsoft Entra admin center (direct link), expand Identity, expand Users, and select All users.

In the blade on the right, search for the user indicated in the results. Select the user account.

In the user blade that opens, select Audit logs. The audit log details of changes made by the user will be shown.
